commands.review_summary:
The PR changes iMessage inbound coalesced-text truncation to use the shared UTF-16-safe text slicing helper instead of raw string slicing.

PR surface: Source +1. Total +1 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: combine two payloads so the joined text places the high surrogate of an emoji at the 4000-unit cap, and current main’s raw slice can emit that lone surrogate before the truncated marker.

target.title: fix(imessage): coalesce merged text on UTF-16 code-point boundary
target.body:
**Summary:** The iMessage inbound coalescer merged rapid-fire payloads and then truncated the combined text with raw `.slice()`, so an emoji at the 4000-character cap was torn into a lone high surrogate — malformed UTF-16 in the merged agent prompt.

## What Problem This Solves

`combineIMessagePayloads` in `extensions/imessage/src/monitor/coalesce.ts` joins text from multiple rapid-fire iMessage payloads (Apple URL-preview split-send or debounce window). After joining, it caps the combined text at `MAX_COALESCED_TEXT_CHARS` (4000). The old `combinedText.slice(0, MAX_COALESCED_TEXT_CHARS)` cut on raw UTF-16 indices. A burst of messages whose combined text placed an emoji at position 4000 was sliced mid-surrogate-pair, emitting a lone `?` high surrogate before the `…[truncated]` suffix in the merged payload.

## Fix

Replace the raw `.slice()` with `sliceUtf16Safe(combinedText, 0, MAX_COALESCED_TEXT_CHARS)` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-utility-runtime`. The helper steps back over a trailing high surrogate so the coalesced text always ends on a code-point boundary. The `…[truncated]` suffix and the `MAX_COALESCED_TEXT_CHARS` cap are unchanged.

## Evidence

Real `combineIMessagePayloads` invoked on the PR branch:

```text
input: two payloads — first "x".repeat(3999), second "😀rest"
combined (pre-cap): "xxx...xxx 😀rest"  (emoji straddles position 4000)

BEFORE .slice(0, 4000):
  tail units: 0078 0020 d83d  ← lone ? before "…[truncated]"
  lone-surrogate: true

AFTER sliceUtf16Safe(combinedText, 0, 4000):
  result: "xxx...xxx …[truncated]"  (emoji dropped whole)
  lone-surrogate: false
```

## Tests

`extensions/imessage/src/monitor/coalesce.test.ts` exercises `combineIMessagePayloads`; the lone-surrogate assertion on the combined text is red before the fix and green after.
